coreIssue: This junior Brand Designer performance conversation is about making the quality bar concrete enough for Carlos to turn feedback into visible design behaviors.
openingQuestion: What does strong work look like to you in your Brand Designer role right now, and where has recent feedback been easiest or hardest to apply?
listenFor: whether he cites a specific project, revision, or critique where he changed his approach
whether he stays general about quality or names concrete signals like craft, rationale, speed, or stakeholder response
if they describe a clear growth blocker such as unclear expectations, tool skills, prioritization, or interpreting feedback
avoid: do not turn the meeting into a list of past misses without defining what better looks like for a junior Brand Designer
do not assume feedback was understood just because he heard it; test for examples of changed behavior
goodOutcome: You and Carlos have agreed what good junior Brand Designer work looks like in one or two concrete areas and one practice he will use on upcoming design work.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick two recent pieces of Carlos's work—one stronger, one weaker—and note the observable behaviors that made each land above or below the current quality bar.
